system: EXPLORER-MAP — the hand-rolled canvas slippy map at the heart of rapp-go / the Explorer. A single vendored, zero-dependency ES module (explorer/tilemap.js) that fetches raster map tiles, draws them to a <canvas>, follows the player's live Geolocation, and lets the other Explorer subsystems (POIs, spawns, catch, AR) pin animated markers onto real-world coordinates. It owns: tile fetch + IndexedDB tile cache (OSM-policy-respecting), Web-Mercator projection, drag/pinch/zoom, the player avatar + accuracy ring + compass heading, marker placement + hit-testing, and the offline/low-GPS/battery behaviour. It reuses the ecosystem's geohash + moment→genome + basket plumbing so a tapped spawn becomes a real .egg.
design:
RECOMMENDATION: hand-rolled canvas raster-tile map, NOT vendored Leaflet. Reasons: (1) A following, marker-heavy slippy map is ~400 lines of Web-Mercator math + drawImage; Leaflet is ~140KB of DOM-layer machinery we'd use 15% of, and its DOM markers can't composite with the ecosystem's canvas software-3D aesthetic. (2) One <canvas> lets creature spawns, the player halo, and the accuracy ring share the exact pulse/breathe motion language of the hologram player — grounded, one system. (3) Full control of the tile fetch queue is what actually lets us honour OSM tile policy (concurrency cap, IDB cache, no prefetch). (4) No build step means a DOM lib is just weight; a hand-rolled module is auditable and forkable like every other surface here.

TILE SOURCE (taste-first, one-line swappable): default to CARTO Positron (light) / dark_matter (dark) — muted, near-monochrome basemaps so the coloured creatures are the only saturated thing on screen (dead-on the "quiet, grounded" brief). Keep OSM-standard fully wired as the guaranteed-policy fallback. A PROVIDERS table: { positron:{url:'https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/light_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png', sub:['a','b','c','d'], max:20, attrib:'© OpenStreetMap contributors © CARTO'}, dark_matter:{...dark_all...}, osm:{url:'https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png', sub:null, max:19, attrib:'© OpenStreetMap contributors'} }. Pick provider by prefers-color-scheme; {r} = '@2x' when devicePixelRatio>1.5 (retina) else ''. Attribution string is drawn on-canvas bottom-right EVERY frame — non-removable.

PROJECTION (Web Mercator / slippy XYZ). worldSize(z)=256*2^z. project(lat,lng,z): px=(lng+180)/360*worldSize; latRad=lat*π/180; py=(1-asinh(tan(latRad))/π)/2*worldSize. Camera state = { centerWorldPx:{x,y}, z:int, scale:float(0.5–2 for smooth pinch between integer tile levels), heading:0 (north-up) or live compass (heading-up, off by default), following:bool }. screenFor(worldPx)= (worldPx - centerWorldPx)*scale + canvasCenter, optionally rotated by heading. unproject inverts it for drag-pan and for tap→latlng.

RENDER. Single <canvas> sized to CSS box × devicePixelRatio. render(now): (a) compute the integer tile range covering the viewport at z; for each {z,x,y} drawImage its decoded ImageBitmap at its screen rect (scaled by `scale`); a tile still decoding draws a 1px subtle grid cell + its parent tile (z-1) upscaled as a placeholder so pans never flash white; a freshly-decoded tile fades in over ~180ms (alpha tween). (b) draw markers sorted by z-index. (c) draw player avatar. (d) draw attribution + a faint scale bar. The loop is rAF-gated: it only runs while `dirty || following || anyMarker.animated || tween-active`; otherwise it sleeps (battery). visibilitychange→pause. Target 30fps cap on the marker/animation path.

TILE PIPELINE. tileKey=`${provider}/${z}/${x}/${y}${r}`. Three tiers: memoryLRU (Map<key,ImageBitmap>, ~200 cap) → IndexedDB (db 'rapp-explorer' v1, store 'tiles' keyPath 'k', value {k, blob, provider, z, fetchedAt}) → network. On miss: enqueue; a fetch pump keeps ≤2 concurrent in-flight, dedupes, and DROPS queued tiles that scrolled out of view before they started. fetch(url,{mode:'cors'})→blob→put IDB (fire-and-forget)→createImageBitmap(blob)→memoryLRU→mark dirty. TTL 30 days for freshness but STALE TILES ARE STILL SERVED offline. Cap requested z at provider.max and at a walking-scale max (18–19). NO speculative prefetch beyond the visible ring (policy).

PLAYER AVATAR + HEADING. setPlayer({lat,lng,accuracy,heading}) is called by watchPosition. Draw: a soft-glow dot (same radial-gradient vocabulary as creature glow), an accuracy ring whose radius = metersToPixels(accuracy, lat) = accuracy/(cos(lat)*156543.03/2^z)*scale, and a heading cone (from DeviceOrientation compass, else GPS coords.heading). WALKING FEEL: never snap — lerp centerWorldPx toward the new fix over ~600ms (ease-out); the accuracy ring gently breathes; on the first fix, fly-to with a short zoom-in tween. A floating "◎ recenter" button appears only when the user has panned away from the player (following=false) and re-locks follow on tap.

MARKERS + HIT-TEST. Public marker: { id, lat, lng, z:0, hitR:22, animated:false, draw(ctx,{x,y},now,map){…}, data }. draw() gets SCREEN coords and paints itself (a POI diamond, a spawn = a small live creature silhouette pulsing). map.addMarker/updateMarker(id,patch)/removeMarker(id). Hit-test on pointerup, only if the pointer moved <8px since pointerdown (else it was a pan): convert markers to screen, iterate by z desc, first with hypot(px-x,py-y) < hitR*scale wins → onTap(marker, latlng); empty space → onTap(null, latlng). The player marker is decorative, never hit-tested.

INPUT. pointerdown/move/up (Pointer Events, unifies touch+mouse): 1-pointer drag → pan (breaks follow), updates centerWorldPx via unproject delta. 2-pointer → pinch: track the pointer midpoint + distance; map distance ratio to `scale`, and when scale crosses ~1.6/0.6 bump the integer z and renormalise scale (so tiles stay ≤max and crisp). Double-tap → zoom-in one level toward the tap point. Wheel (desktop) → zoom. +/− buttons for accessibility.

LOW-GPS / INDOOR / NO-SIGNAL. watchPosition(enableHighAccuracy:true, maximumAge:5000, timeout:15000). If accuracy>50m: dim the ring, show "finding you…", and the map still follows the coarse fix (spawn/POI systems widen their range tolerance accordingly). If no fix within timeout: fall back to lastKnown (persisted to localStorage 'explorer.lastFix') and show a "location is hazy" chip; if never any fix, drop the map on lastKnown or a neutral world view with a "grant location to explore" prompt. Offline/no tiles: cached tiles from IDB render; missing tiles show the grid placeholder tinted faintly ("off the map") — caught creatures, cached POIs, and the whole catch loop keep working with zero network.

BATTERY. The high-accuracy watch is the real cost, so: pause the watch AND rAF on visibilitychange:hidden; when the player is detected stationary (successive fixes <10m apart for ~30s) downgrade from continuous watch to a 20s getCurrentPosition poll, upgrading back on the first movement; DeviceOrientation listener attached only while the map view is on-screen; fps-cap + idle-sleep on the render loop as above; retina @2x tiles only above dpr 1.5.
build: explorer/tilemap.js (map engine, zero-dep) + explorer/geo.js (watchPosition controller) + explorer/moment.js (vendored geohash/momentToGenome/genomeId/open-meteo/keepToBasket) + explorer/index.html (canvas shell) + explorer/player.html (re-vendored) + explorer/sw.js + manifest.webmanifest. Web-Mercator XYZ math by hand (worldSize=256*2^z; py uses asinh(tan(latRad))). Tiles: CARTO light_all/dark_all default (retina {r}=@2x), OSM standard fallback, one PROVIDERS table. Cache: memory LRU→IndexedDB 'rapp-explorer'/'tiles'→fetch({mode:'cors'})→createImageBitmap. Fetch pump ≤2 concurrent, drop off-screen, no prefetch, cap z≤provider.max. Single canvas, rAF gated by dirty|following|animated, visibility-paused. Markers carry a draw(ctx,screenXY,now) callback; hit-test on pointerup with <8px move + hitR. Player from watchPosition(highAccuracy) with stationary-downgrade + lastFix persistence; heading from DeviceOrientation (iOS requestPermission on gesture). GitHub Pages HTTPS satisfies geolocation/orientation/getUserMedia secure-context.
integration: .egg FORMAT: the map itself only stores {lat,lng} on markers; the moment→egg conversion uses the ecosystem's own momentToGenome + genomeId + cart shape verbatim (vendored in moment.js), so every caught spawn is a byte-identical hologram-cartridge/1.0 to what the Weather Cabinet mints — id=sha256(canonical genome).slice(12), born.coord=`<geohash9>·<epochMs>`, born.from=`live …`. GEOHASH: reuses geohashEncode/Decode exactly (already the coord grammar the whole ecosystem parses in momentPhrase/bornDate). WEATHER: reuses the same open-meteo current endpoint (latitude,longitude,current=temperature_2m,weathercode,wind_speed_10m,is_day&timezone=auto), so a spawn is literally born from THAT place's live sky at the moment of the tap — the soul of the brief. BASKET/CABINET/COMPANION LOOP: caught eggs go through keepToBasket() into the shared 'rapp-basket' IDB store (db v1, store 'eggs', record {id,egg,title,born,addedAt}) — the same store the Cabinet's kept-strip and the Basket read, and from which eggs flow to breeding and the Companion. DEEP-LINKS unchanged: an encounter can hand off via CABINET '#remix='+b64url(JSON(cart)), LANTERN '#'+b64url(...), or companion player.html '#'+b64url(...) — same b64url helper (btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent))→url-safe) used across hologram/companion. PLAYER: spawn/AR views render the creature through the re-vendored player.html postMessage handshake (load-cartridge→hatched), identical to run.html/companion. WHAT I EXPOSE to the sibling systems: map.addMarker/updateMarker/removeMarker + the on('tap') event + project/unproject + metersToPixels — the POI system (Overpass) drops POI markers, the spawn system drops creature markers, the catch/AR systems consume the tapped marker's {lat,lng} + egg cart. The map is the shared coordinate surface; it does not own POIs, catch RNG, or AR — it just places and hit-tests their markers on real ground.
mvp: A single explorer/index.html that: (1) creates the canvas map on CARTO Positron/dark_matter tiles with the IndexedDB tile cache working (pan around offline after a first load); (2) starts watchPosition and follows the player as a breathing halo + accuracy ring + heading cone, with drag-pan breaking follow and a ◎ recenter button restoring it, pinch/double-tap zoom, and on-canvas attribution; (3) handles the unhappy paths — permission denied → lastFix + prompt, poor accuracy → dimmed ring, offline → cached tiles + faint 'off the map' grid; (4) proves the full soul-loop end to end with ONE hard-coded test spawn marker placed ~20m from the player: tapping it calls the vendored open-meteo fetch at that coord, runs momentToGenome + genomeId to mint a real hologram-cartridge/1.0 egg (born.coord=geohash·epoch, born.from='live …'), keepToBasket()s it into 'rapp-basket', and opens it in the re-vendored player.html — so before POI/catch/AR exist, walking up to a spawn and keeping THAT place's live sky as a real, breedable, talkable .egg already works. Later the sibling systems just swap the one test marker for Overpass POIs + real spawns via the same addMarker/on('tap') API.
ontaste: Muted monochrome basemap so the only saturated, moving things are the creatures born from your real sky — the map recedes, the moment is the subject. Camera lerps (never snaps) for a walking feel; player is a soft breathing halo matching the hologram glow language, not an arcade icon. Persistent on-frame OSM/CARTO attribution + rate-capping is the honest, on-taste treatment of real place data. Offline degrades to a faint 'off the map' grid, never an error. Restraint honours the keepsake premise: these are real places you walked to, not loot.
risks: OSM/CARTO tile-usage policy: honour it with the IDB cache, ≤2 concurrent fetches, no speculative prefetch, capped max-zoom, and permanent on-canvas attribution; keep the provider a one-line swap and lead with CARTO's muted basemaps (their free tier still requires OSM+CARTO credit). If volume ever grows, the same table swaps to a self-hostable/keyed source without touching the engine.
Tile CORS + decode: both providers send ACAO:* so fetch({mode:'cors'})→createImageBitmap works and the canvas stays untainted; guard createImageBitmap on very old Safari with an +drawImage fallback.

Geolocation permission denied / hazy indoor fixes: never hard-fail — fall back to persisted lastFix, show a 'location is hazy' chip, keep cached tiles + already-caught creatures fully usable; spawn/POI range tolerances widen when accuracy is poor.

Battery drain from enableHighAccuracy watch: pause watch+rAF on tab-hidden, downgrade to a 20s poll when stationary, cap fps, idle-sleep the render loop, attach DeviceOrientation only while the map is visible.

iOS quirks: DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission() needs a user gesture (wire it to the recenter/compass button); getUserMedia/orientation/geolocation all require a secure context — GitHub Pages HTTPS satisfies this, but the compass needs the gesture and Low-Power Mode throttles rAF (accept graceful degradation).
IndexedDB quota / Safari private mode: LRU-evict the tile store by fetchedAt, wrap all IDB in try/catch so a full or blocked DB degrades to memory-only + live network instead of crashing the map.

Vendored-copy drift: moment.js and player.html are verbatim lifts of hologram source; header each with 'source:' and register with the ecosystem-sync/neuron-swarm skills so a future momentToGenome change is caught (this repo already re-vendors player.html per cycle, so the pattern and its risk are known).
